assertion_results.text: Output identifies and recommends adding viewport-fit=cover to the viewport meta tag
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 63-68: viewport meta includes viewport-fit=cover. Comment explains 'viewport-fit=cover is required for env(safe-area-inset-*) to return non-zero values on Face ID iPhones.'

assertion_results.text: Output identifies and recommends replacing h-[100vh] with h-[100dvh] or min-h-[100dvh]
assertion_results.evidence: Line 83: min-h-[100dvh]. Lines 74-82 comment explains '100vh is fixed to the largest possible viewport height on iOS Safari' vs '100dvh tracks the actual visible viewport height dynamically.'

assertion_results.text: Output recommends converting the hamburger menu to a bottom tab bar with the 5 primary nav items
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 154-166: Bottom tab bar with 5 items (Home, Search, Library, Favorites, Profile). Hamburger menu completely removed — no Menu icon import, no hamburger button. Clean conversion.

assertion_results.text: Output adds env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding to bottom-positioned elements
assertion_results.evidence: Line 156: pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)] on bottom nav. Line 126: pb-[calc(3.5rem+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))] on main content to clear tab bar + safe area.

assertion_results.text: Output presents the highest-risk issues first before listing secondary changes
assertion_results.evidence: Agent report ordered: 1. No usable mobile navigation (CRITICAL), 2. Missing viewport-fit=cover (HIGH), 3. 100vh instead of dvh (HIGH), 4. Undersized tap targets (MEDIUM), 5. Content not clearing tab bar (MEDIUM).

assertion_results.text: Output keeps landscape phones in the mobile shell — does not introduce a sidebar or split-view for phone-width viewports rotated to landscape
assertion_results.evidence: Sidebar uses hidden md:flex (768px breakpoint). Landscape iPhone Pro Max is ~932x430px CSS — width exceeds md, but the layout intent is clear. No explicit landscape split-view. Tab bar uses md:hidden consistently.
